identifier: ISBN 978-0-8157-0111-8 (2nd ed.; 1st ed. Yale University Press, 1982)
summary: The standard mathematical treatment of apportionment: dividing a fixed integer total into parts proportional to weights. Defines and analyzes Hamilton's largest-remainder method — give each part the floor of its exact proportional share, then distribute the leftover units to the parts with the largest fractional remainders — which by construction always sums exactly to the total. Also establishes the method's properties and paradoxes relative to divisor methods.
title: Fair Representation: Meeting the Ideal of One Man, One Vote
